contexts.context: DN: Exactly, because some functions are <mark>useless</mark> without lazy evaluation. I think we as a…
…paternalism. Like forbidding multiplying by zero because it’s <mark>useless</mark>.
…can also use it on maps which is probably <mark>useless</mark>, but logically consistent.
…if it allows users to do things that are <mark>useless</mark>.
…paternal. It means the system will reject some <mark>useless</mark> types.
